notes: Score holding steady at 3.6. The office view is genuinely fun — the character SVGs, speech bubbles, and animations give it real personality. But there are recurring issues I keep flagging that haven't been addressed: mobile responsiveness is still rough, accessibility gaps remain, and the CSS has a syntax error. The task tags and error halo from last cycle are nice additions though.

suggestions.title: CSS syntax error — orphaned property block in App.css
suggestions.description: At the bottom of App.css there's an orphaned CSS block starting with 'background: var(--surface2);' that's not inside any selector (around the .skeleton class). This is a parse error that could cause downstream styles to break or be ignored. The .skeleton class definition is missing its selector. Should be '.skeleton { background: var(--surface2); border-radius: var(--radius); animation: shimmer 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; }'

suggestions.title: No loading state for Office tab
suggestions.description: The Dashboard tab has a nice LoadingSkeleton component, but the Office tab has no loading indicator at all. When the page first loads and data is being fetched, the office view renders with zero workers and no visual feedback. Users see an empty office floor with just Bloberto until data arrives. Add a loading state or skeleton to the Office view.

suggestions.title: Tab toggle buttons missing focus styles
suggestions.description: The tab toggle and activity filter buttons have hover styles but no visible focus indicators. Keyboard users can't tell which button is focused. Add :focus-visible outlines to .tab-toggle button and .activity-filter-bar button.

suggestions.title: Polling continues when tab is idle for 10+ minutes but resumes without check
suggestions.description: The IDLE_TIMEOUT stops polling after 10 minutes of inactivity, which is great. But when the user comes back and moves the mouse, polling doesn't immediately resume — it waits for the next interval tick. Should trigger an immediate sync on activity resume after idle timeout.

suggestions.title: Activity log filter state not preserved across tab switches
suggestions.description: If you select 'Completions' filter in the activity log, switch to Office tab, then switch back to Dashboard, the filter resets to 'All'. Minor but annoying for users who are tracking specific event types.

suggestions.title: No roster view — can't see team members who aren't active
suggestions.description: The ghost characters in the office view hint at roster members, but there's no way to see the full team roster with roles, hire dates, etc. A simple 'Team' section or third tab showing all roster members (active or not) would add value. Currently roster data is fetched but only used for ghost placement.

suggestions.title: CharacterAvatar has massive if-else chain for each worker
suggestions.description: The CharacterAvatar component in Office.jsx has individual if blocks for bloberto, carlos, maya, dave, sofia, and luna — each with ~20 lines of inline SVG. This doesn't scale. If we add more team members, this file will balloon. Consider a data-driven approach where character features (hair color, skin tone, accessories) are stored in worker data and rendered by a generic avatar builder.

suggestions.title: Footer quote feels stale
suggestions.description: The footer always shows 'If it compiles, ship it.' — would be more fun if it rotated through a few Bloberto-isms, similar to the hover speech bubbles. Small delight that reinforces the personality.

suggestions.title: No way to see historical suggestions/scores
suggestions.description: Each QA review overwrites suggestions.json. There's no history of how scores have changed over time. Consider appending to an array or using timestamped files (suggestions-2026-03-07.json) so we can track improvement trends.

suggestions.title: Coffee corner is decorative-only
suggestions.description: The coffee corner in the office view is cute but doesn't do anything. Could show idle workers hanging out there instead of wandering randomly, or display a fun 'coffees consumed today' counter based on activity count. Small touch that would make the office feel more alive.
